description: Iran's Ministry of Petroleum (Vezarat-e Naft) manages the National Iranian Oil Company (NIOC) and oversees all upstream and downstream oil, gas, and petrochemical activities. Oil revenue has historically funded the majority of the state budget. Key ministers: Gholam Reza Aghazadeh (1985–1997), [Bijan Namdar Zanganeh](/people/bijan-namdar-zanganeh) (1997–2005, 2013–2021), Kazem Vaziri Hamaneh (2005–2008), Gholamhossein Nozari (2008–2009), Masoud Mir-Kazemi (2009–2011), [Rostam Ghasemi](/people/rostam-ghasemi) (2011–2013), [Javad Owji](/people/javad-owji) (2021–2024), [Mohsen Paknejad](/people/mohsen-paknejad) (2024–present).
editorial_notes: One of the most powerful economic ministries. NIOC is one of the world's largest oil companies. The minister oversees the strategic energy sector.
